sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम्। उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति॥
transliteration: bhīṣma-droṇa-pramukhataḥ sarveṣāṃ ca mahī-kṣitām uvāca pārtha paśyaitān samavetān kurūn iti
literal_meaning: In front of Bhishma, Drona and all the rulers of the earth, Hrishikesha said: 'O Partha, behold these Kurus assembled here.'
contextual_meaning: Krishna, acting as both witness and mirror, positions Arjuna to confront the totality of his relational world—teachers, kin, authority figures—compelling him to see the battlefield as an externalization of his own divided psyche. The command 'behold' is an invitation to radical self-recognition: every face arrayed against him embodies an inner voice, value or attachment that must now be owned or released.
psychological_state: The moment of acute moral vertigo when every familiar anchor of right action simultaneously claims allegiance, freezing the agent in panoramic dread.
trigger_scenario: A star employee must decide whether to expose the mentor who trained them and the company that raised them after discovering systemic fraud, seeing their face on every corporate video screen during the crisis meeting.
anchor_text: Face every inner conflict you project onto others.
